listitem.para: Sets the type of SA that will be produced. Valid options are: &esp; for encryption and integrity protection, &ah; for integrity protection only.
Instead of specifying both phase2= and phase2alg=, the parameters ah= and esp= can be used to directly specify the Child (IPsec) SA's protocol and cryptographic algorithms.

Historic note: The very first IPsec designs called for use of AH plus ESP to offer authentication, integrity and confidentiality. That dual protocol use was a significant burden, so ESP was extended to offer all three services, and AH remained as an auth/integ. The old mode of ah+esp is no longer supported in compliance with RFC 8221 Section 4. Additionally, AH does not play well with NATs, so it is strongly recommended to use ESP with the null cipher if you require unencrypted authenticated transport.

Historic note: the term Phase 2 dates back to the original &IKEv1; specificiation where the Child (IPsec) SA was established using a second (Phase 2) exchange; since &IKEv2; can establish a Child SA during the initial exchange the term no longer applies.
History: ah= and esp= were made fully functional in &Libreswan; 5.3, prior to this they were aliases for phase2alg=.
